channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: A Texas execution is renewing calls for clemency. It's rarely granted
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — A Texas man set to die this month is at the center of another push for clemency in the U.S., this time backed by several GOP lawmakers and bestselling author John Grisham, who say a father’s 2002 conviction for killing his infant daughter deserves a second look.
item.dc:creator: NADIA LATHAN and SUMMER BALLENTINE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Idaho state senator tells Native American candidate 'go back where you came from' in forum
item.description: KENDRICK, Idaho (AP) — Tensions rose during a bipartisan forum this week after an audience question about discrimination reportedly led an Idaho state senator to angrily tell a Native American candidate to “go back where you came from.” Republican Sen.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bands drop out of Orlando music festival after Kyle Rittenhouse booked as guest
item.description: Shell Shock organizers describe the festival as a concert charity event that seeks to raise funds for a charity that "supports first responders struggling with injuries to include Post Traumatic Stress Disorder (PTSD).
item.dc:creator: Gabriel Castillo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Former owner of water buffalo that roamed Iowa suburb for days pleads guilty
item.description: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — The owner of a water buffalo that ran loose in a Des Moines, Iowa, suburb for days pleaded guilty for having an animal at large. The owner was fined $105 and court costs Thursday, the Des Moines Register reported.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Ex- Virginia cop who killed shoplifting suspect acquitted of manslaughter, guilty on firearm charge
item.description: FAIRFAX, Va. (AP) — A jury on Friday acquitted a former police officer in Virginia of involuntary manslaughter after he fatally shot a shoplifting suspect outside a busy shopping mall.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BARAKAT and OLIVIA DIAZ, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: When will the power return? Weary Carolinas residents long for relief after Helene's fury
item.description: ASHEVILLE, N.C. (AP) — The weary and worn residents of Julianne Johnson's neighborhood in Asheville have been getting by without electricity since Hurricane Helene tore through the Southeast last week and upended their lives.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Ohio woman's viral TikTok videos lead prompt search for body in her yard
item.description: Police in Columbus, Ohio have concluded an investigation after a woman posted on TikTok that she might have discovered a body buried in her yard.
item.dc:creator: Katie Millard, Fox 5

item.title: Los Angeles mayor picks ex-sheriff to be city's police chief
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Former LA County Sheriff Jim McDonnell has been selected to lead the Los Angeles Police Department, taking charge of the force of nearly 9,000 officers as discontent grows among the city's residents over public safety even as violent crime numbers drop, the mayor announced Friday.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New judge could give Diddy another attempt to win bail
item.description: While Diddy's legal team hasn’t commented, the assignment of a new judge potentially opens the door for a third bail hearing.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What renters should know about breaking a lease to buy a home
item.description: Renters can break their lease to purchase their own home but they should understand the ramifications of leaving early
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Some perplexed at jury's mixed verdict in trial for 3 former officers in Tyre Nichols' death
item.description: The trial of three former Memphis officers charged in the fatal beating of Tyre Nichols ended with a mixed verdict, surprising and perplexing some observers who questioned why the jury would acquit two of the officers on the most serious charges.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Sainz And Jonathan Mattise, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: U.S. adds more jobs, unemployment dips to 4.1%
item.description: BAKERSFIELD, Calif. (KGET) -- The U.S. added 254,000 jobs in September, while the unemployment rate fell from 4.2% in August to 4.1%. The unemployment rate was expected to be unchanged from August.
item.dc:creator: Luis Garcia, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Why are there still so many blackouts after major storms?
item.description: The high costs of infrastructure improvements likely have contributed to the blackouts. Experts say more can be done to prevent power outages, but it would require significant investments.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, KRON4

item.title: Satellite shows before-and-after flooding in North Carolina after Helene
item.description: Planet Labs captured and compared satellite images that show the storm's devastating impact on one of the hardest hit cities.
item.dc:creator: Jesse Ullmann, WFLA, NBC

item.title: McDonald's adding Chicken Big Mac to US menus for a limited time
item.description: McDonald's is set to introduce the Chicken Big Mac, a reimagined version of its classic Big Mac, featuring two tempura-battered chicken patties on a sesame seed bun, on Oct. 10.
item.dc:creator: Nick Gladney, KRON4

item.title: North Carolina is distributing Benadryl and EpiPens as yellow jackets swarm from Helene flooding
item.description: Deadly flooding from Hurricane Helene in western North Carolina has also disrupted the underground nests of yellow jackets, bees and other insects, causing them to swarm and sting people struggling to recover from the storm.
item.dc:creator: DEVI SHASTRI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What is Marburg virus? Outbreak of Ebola-like disease detected in yet another country
item.description: Health officials in Rwanda are dealing with the country’s first outbreak of the Marburg virus, an Ebola-like disease which, if left untreated, has a fatality rate of up to 88%.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: House Republican apologizes for wearing blackface at 2006 Halloween party
item.description: Rep. Mike Lawler (R-N.Y.) apologized late Thursday night for wearing blackface as part of a Halloween costume portraying pop star Michael Jackson in 2006 during a college party.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Musk to attend Trump rally at site of attempted assassination
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk said he plans to attend former President Donald Trump’s rally in battleground Pennsylvania this weekend when Trump returns to the site where he survived the first assassination attempt on his life.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KRON4

item.title: 5,000 gallons of hot asphalt spilled on Texas interstate
item.description: "We ask for you to keep the victim and their family in your thoughts and prayers," the Lindale Fire Department said.
item.dc:creator: Daisy Escatel, NewsNation

item.title: Milwaukee man accused of stealing from same Walgreens 3 separate times
item.description: John Dzwonkowski faces charges of stealing toilet paper, soaps, lotion, razors and household cleaning items.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Two human cases of bird flu confirmed in California as infection continues to spread in US: CDC
item.description: The Centers of Disease Control confirmed on Thursday that two additional cases of the bird flu were identified in California, upping the number to 16 cases in 2024.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mixed verdict for 3 Memphis officers convicted in Tyre Nichols' fatal beating
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — Three former Memphis police officers were convicted Thursday in the 2023 fatal beating of Tyre Nichols, but were acquitted of the harshest charges they faced for a death that sparked national protests and calls for broad changes in policing.
item.dc:creator: ADRIAN SAINZ, KRISTIN M. HALL and JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Springsteen calls Trump 'most dangerous candidate for president in my lifetime,' backs Harris
item.description: The rock legend said the former president “doesn’t understand the meaning of this country, its history or what it means to be deeply American.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Earth's 2nd moon has arrived: What you need to know
item.description: A second moon has officially entered Earth's orbit—sort of. Although it's being called a "mini-moon," it's actually an asteroid named 2024 PT5. The asteroid has been temporarily captured by Earth's gravitational pull. The asteroid will remain in Earth's orbit from now until Nov.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Lewis, KTLA 5

item.title: Rhode Island woman works 350 hours to build first home
item.description: "It makes you excited to think that you're going to build your own home and help others to get theirs," she told NewsNatoin affiliate WPRI. "That's like a dream come true.
item.dc:creator: Catie McNeil, NewsNation

item.title: Man accused of trying to carjack pregnant woman in Connecticut drive-thru
item.description: With their help, police were able to arrest the suspect, who police say is facing a multitude of charges in this case and others.
item.dc:creator: Bailey Wright, NewsNation

item.title: Navy veteran student recounts saving his ASU classmate from stabbing attack: 'Compelled to do something'
item.description: An Arizona State University student sprang into action to help classmate during "random" stabbing attack on the campus, saying that he felt "compelled" to help.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dockworkers' union to suspend strike until Jan. 15
item.description: The union representing 45,000 striking U.S. dockworkers at East and Gulf coast ports reached a deal Thursday to suspend a three-day strike until Jan. 15 to provide time to negotiate a new contract.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Subway rider shot in the head by police files claim accusing officers of recklessly opening fire
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A lawyer for a New York City subway rider who was inadvertently shot in the head by police at a station has filed a legal claim against the city, accusing the officers of showing “carelessness and reckless” disregard for the lives of others when they opened fire on a man holding
item.dc:creator: CEDAR ATTANASIO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 3 officers convicted in Tyre Nichols fatal beating, 2 acquitted of civil rights charges
item.description: Three former Memphis officers were convicted Thursday of charges of witness tampering in the 2023 fatal beating of Tyre Nichols, and two were acquitted of federal civil rights violations in a death that sparked national protests and calls for broad changes in policing.
item.dc:creator: Megan Fayard, WGN 9

item.title: Los Angeles D.A. to ‘reevaluate’ Menendez brothers’ convictions
item.description: Los Angeles County District Attorney George Gascón provided an update Thursday on the convictions of Erik and Lyle Menendez, who were convicted of killing their parents in August 1989.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, WGN 9

item.title: Parents spot kids in 7-Eleven robbery footage, turn them in to police
item.description: In each instance, the crew rolled up to the stores on bicycles during the evening hours and stole items, damaged property and left without paying, police said.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, WFLA, NBC

item.title: VIDEO: Helene waves and storm surge crash into Florida home as owners watch
item.description: Terrifying moments caught on video showed Hurricane Helene’s powerful and deadly storm surge as it crashed into a home in Pinellas County last week.
item.dc:creator: Georgia McCarthur, Fox 5

item.title: Hurricane Helene scrambles politics in 3 battleground states
item.description: The 500-mile-long path of destruction cut by Hurricane Helene has scrambled the politics of three battleground states that could determine control of the White House and Senate: North Carolina, Georgia and Florida.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, WFLA, NBC

item.title: South Carolina grandparents killed by fallen tree during Helene are found hugging
item.description: A South Carolina couple were found hugging each other after a tree fell on their bedroom and killed them.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Push to map Great Lakes bottom gains momentum amid promises effort will help fishing and shipping
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — Jennifer Boehme grew up scouting beaches around her home in St. Petersburg, Florida, for whatever she could find. Rocks, sand dollars, coquina mollusks — anything the ocean gave up. Now, 40 years later, Boehme wants to launch another treasure hunt.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND, Associated Press, ABC 4
